title: Undercut/overcut opportunity proxy from relative pace and traffic window
summary: F1 strategy analysis emphasizes that pit timing depends not only on tyre age but on whether fresh tyres create an undercut advantage or whether cold-tyre conditions favor an overcut.
rationale: F1 strategy analysis emphasizes that pit timing depends not only on tyre age but on whether fresh tyres create an undercut advantage or whether cold-tyre conditions favor an overcut. That interaction is not captured by fixed finish-window heuristics alone.
implementation_hint: Build race-lap relative features such as driver LapTime_Delta minus same-race-lap median, position gap proxies from Position and Position_Change, and track-specific warmup/degradation regime flags by Race x Compound x early/late stint. Add features that estimate whether pitting now likely improves relative pace next lap versus staying out one more lap.
expected_effect: Improves discrimination in borderline windows where two drivers have similar tyre life but only one has a real tactical reason to pit immediately.
risk: Same-lap aggregates are leakage-prone if computed on the full dataset or using future rows from the same race in validation.
